pi.name: Prof. Beatrice Ombuki-Berman
pi.title: Professor, Department of Computer Science · Brock University
pi.bio: Professor Ombuki-Berman is a full professor in the Department of Computer Science at Brock University. Her research spans bio-inspired algorithms, combinatorial optimization, swarm intelligence, and multi-objective evolutionary computation. She has published extensively in leading journals and conferences including IEEE TEVC, Swarm and Evolutionary Computation, and GECCO.

students.name: Rasa Khosrowshahli
students.focus: I am a CS PhD student at Brock University. I became interested in evolutionary algorithms and their applications in ML. My work focuses on neuroevolution with parameter efficiency through dimensionality reduction and subspace optimization. Previously, I attended Ontario Tech University, where I earned Master's degree in Computer Engineering in one-year. In my leisure time, I enjoy playing volleyball, football (soccer), running early in the morning, and playing board games with friends.

students.name: Stephen Asobiela
students.focus: Stephen Yamzuuga Asobiela is a PhD student with over 15 years of industry experience designing and delivering scalable, cloud-native enterprise systems across fintech, supply chain, and digital banking domains, having held senior and technical lead roles at organizations including ECI Software Solutions, Infios Inc., and Meridian Credit Union. He holds an MSc in Computer Science from Brock University, Canada, and a BSc (First Class Honours) from Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology, Ghana. His doctoral research focuses on the intersection of evolutionary computation, reinforcement learning, and machine unlearning, with emphasis on developing efficient, privacy-aware learning systems. Stephen's extensive background in building high-performance, large-scale software systems uniquely informs his research on optimizing and governing modern deep learning frameworks. Outside of academia and work, he enjoys watching and playing soccer and spending quality time with his family.

students.name: Michael Eshun
students.focus: My research interests lie at the intersection of artificial intelligence, optimization, healthcare logistics, and software engineering. I am particularly interested in combinatorial optimization problems such as vehicle routing, scheduling, and resource allocation, with a focus on healthcare applications like the Home Health Care Routing and Scheduling Problem. My work explores the use of metaheuristic algorithms, including genetic algorithms, multi-objective optimization, and hybrid search methods, to solve complex real-world decision-making problems. I am also interested in applied AI, data-driven systems, software development, and building scalable digital platforms that improve operational efficiency and user experience.

students.name: Kaija Sproxton
students.focus: She is interested in Evolutionary Algorithms for feature selection and dimensionality reduction, with a specific interest in integrating similarity-aware approaches. She aims to explore how human-in-the-loop methodologies can further optimize and refine these evolutionary processes.

interests: Optimizing Training and Inference for Constrained Hardware
students.name: Dimitar Indovski
students.focus: He is interested in Agentic AI, Robotics, and Optimizing Training and Inference for Constrained Hardware. He is currently working on a project to optimize training and inference for constrained hardware.

students.name: Jose Henriquez
students.focus: I was born in Colombia and moved to Canada in 2008, I completed 3 years of Biology at the University of Waterloo before transferring to brock for Computing and Business, My research interests include Robotics, Optimization, including my biology knowledge in my work and lately i've done a project on cross-hospital fairness in chest X-rays that got accepted to IEEE CIBCB Conference. my thesis topic is still not fully decided. My interests outside school are Golf, Riding my Yamaha Motorcycle and watching movies.

students.name: QiQi Gao
students.focus: MSc in CS focused on discovering more efficient methods in ML, especially in LLMs. Past works include early research in prompting and prompt-based methods in LLMs, specifically auto-regressive models like GPT-3. Outside of formal research, I'm generally interested in developments of tech and science. My personal hobbies includes, games, cinema, and music.

students.name: Gideon Oludeyi
students.focus: Gideon is a MSc student candidate at Brock University. His research interest lies in stochastic home healthcare routing and scheduling problem. Outside of research, he likes listening to music and doing software development.

students.name: Alec Petrovic
students.focus: His research interests focus on making meta-heuristic optimization more efficient and practical, by understanding how parameter configurations affect algorithm performance and using surrogate models to accelerate tuning. Meta-heuristic optimization algorithms (Differential Evolution and Particle Swarm Optimization) and their search dynamics. Parameter tuning and configuration using the Parameter Configuration Landscape (PCL). Surrogate modeling with Artificial Neural Networks to approximate the PCL and reduce computational cost.

students.name: Elias Switzer
students.focus: I'm going into my 5th year of my undergrad in computer science. My research interest is swarm intelligence but my actual summer research project is still TBA. Some of my past projects in AI include PSO for Neural Architecture Search and Applications of Graph Neural Networks. Some of my hobbies outside of research include video games, rock climbing, and piano. I also love to travel and the coolest place I've visited is Japan.

students.name: Cole Corbett
students.focus: Cole Corbett is a Computer Science graduate student with research interests in artificial intelligence and machine learning. Outside of research, he enjoys staying involved in local and provincial politics, umpiring baseball, and managing his own self-hosted dedicated server, where he explores networking, systems administration, and new technologies.
